tickerText: [TENNIS] Grass-court tune-up weekend: Linda Noskova tops Jessica Pegula 6-4, 4-6, 6-3 in a rain-delayed Berlin final for her first WTA 500 title and a projected top-10 debut; Francisco Cerundolo outlasts Tommy Paul 6-7(4), 6-4, 6-3 in the longest Queen's Club final ever; Frances Tiafoe becomes the first American Halle champion with a 6-4, 6-4 win over Taylor Fritz • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals in Paris: Diego Elias becomes the first South American season champion, rallying past Paul Coll 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9; world No. 1 Hania El Hammamy takes her seventh title of 2025/26 with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 win over Sivasangari Subramaniam • [BADMINTON] Macau Open closes at the East Asian Games Dome: Hu Zhe An stages an 11-21, 21-10, 21-13 comeback against Kantaphon Wangcharoen for his first Super 300 crown; Kim Ga Eun beats Park Ga Eun 21-16, 21-13; Korea's Jin Yong and Lee Jong Min take men's doubles gold • [PICKLEBALL] Capital Securities Beijing Open crowns champions at China's National Tennis Center: Jack Wong Hong-kit defeats Zane Ford 11-5, 11-8 in men's singles; Sahra Dennehy wins women's singles and doubles but is denied a triple crown in mixed • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana at Hala Tivoli: Shunsuke Togami beats Hugo Calderano for men's singles gold and pairs with Hiroto Shinozuka for doubles; Hina Hayata tops Zhu Yuling in a seven-game women's final and doubles with Miwa Harimoto; Korea's Lim Jong-hoon and Shin Yu-bin take mixed doubles

newsItems.title: Grass Season Peaks as Noskova, Cerundolo, and Tiafoe Take Titles
newsItems.excerpt: Wimbledon tune-up weekend delivered three champions on Sunday. Linda Noskova beat Jessica Pegula 6-4, 4-6, 6-3 in a rain-delayed Berlin final for her first WTA 500 title and a projected top-10 debut. Francisco Cerundolo rallied past Tommy Paul 6-7(4), 6-4, 6-3 in the longest Queen's Club final on record to become the first Argentine men's champion at the HSBC Championships. Frances Tiafoe made American history at Halle, defeating Taylor Fritz 6-4, 6-4 for his first ATP 500 crown and fourth career title.

newsItems.title: Elias and El Hammamy Crown PSA Tour Finals in Paris
newsItems.excerpt: The 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour season ended at Centquatre-Paris with two champions sharing $108,000 each. Peru's Diego Elias became the first South American to win the season finale, coming from a game down to beat Paul Coll 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 in 82 minutes for his 23rd tour title. World No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claimed her second PSA Tour Finals crown and seventh title this season with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 victory over Malaysia's Sivasangari Subramaniam.

newsItems.title: Wong and Dennehy Dominate Beijing Open at Olympic Venue
newsItems.excerpt: PPA Tour Asia's Capital Securities Beijing Open concluded at China's National Tennis Center with Jack Wong Hong-kit defeating top seed Zane Ford 11-5, 11-8 for men's singles gold. Australia's Sahra Dennehy overwhelmed Chao Yi Wang 11-7, 11-3 in the women's final and later paired with Yufei Long for doubles gold, but Wang and Len Yang denied her a triple crown with an 11-7, 11-6 mixed-doubles win in the Olympic Green venue's pickleball debut.

newsItems.title: Japan Sweeps WTT Star Contender Ljubljana
newsItems.excerpt: Japan claimed four of five titles as the WTT Star Contender Ljubljana closed at Hala Tivoli. Shunsuke Togami beat Hugo Calderano in a five-game men's singles final and teamed with Hiroto Shinozuka to edge China's Wen Ruibo and Huang Youzheng 3-2 in doubles. Hina Hayata survived a seven-game thriller against Zhu Yuling for women's singles gold and paired with Miwa Harimoto for doubles. Korea's Lim Jong-hoon and Shin Yu-bin were the lone non-Japanese champions in mixed doubles.
